behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at the embedding-model -> vector-index handoff boundary, not a single local function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement by itself. LLM-DEP: yes, execution must call a real embedding model API to ensure actual output shape. PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior phrase: "embedding model to convert the query into a vector" + "correct dimensionality for the vector database index." Adjacent lower level miss (Integration): a stub embedder returning the expected length always passes, but misses real failures like model/version drift changing output dimensions. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior requires one model decision/output shape, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the production-configured embedding model with a real query, read the active index dimension from the vector DB schema/config, and assert returned vector length equals index dimension.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is primarily module handoff (query embedding/search layer/vector-store adapter), where ordering bugs appear in wiring (e.g., ascending vs descending). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess if using local/in-process vector store fixture; (b) no dependence on AI output quality; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real HTTP/network/OS effects; Agentic rejected because correctness here is score ordering logic, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test vector index with known vectors and expected cosine scores, run the real similarity-search path (service + repo adapter), and assert returned scores are monotonic descending.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Could be System in deployments where only remote managed vector DB is available; Integration is minimum if local DB fixture exists.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A mock reranker can only replay fixed reorderings and cannot validate nuanced intent handling. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real LLM ranking decisions for nuanced queries. PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior phrase: "LLM re-ranking step changes the order ... when the query has nuanced intent." Adjacent lower level miss (Integration): deterministic stub reranker can be set to reorder, so test passes even if real model fails to detect nuance and preserves bad ordering. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because this behavior validates one agentic decision point (rerank), not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Run retrieval to get raw top-K, then call the real LLM reranker on nuanced-intent query fixtures; assert at least one rank position changes versus raw order for expected cases.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Whether a query is truly "no close match" depends on real embedding-space behavior; synthetic stubs can hide noisy nearest neighbors. LLM-DEP: behavior depends on real AI/ML embedding output distribution (and any semantic filtering tied to it). PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior phrase: "query with no close matches ... returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise." Adjacent lower level miss (Integration): fixed fake vectors can enforce clean separation and always produce empty results, missing real-model drift/noise that yields spurious near neighbors. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because this behavior can be validated with one agentic stage outcome criterion (match/no-match), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real embedding model with an unrelated corpus and out-of-domain queries, execute actual search path, and assert API returns empty results when similarity never crosses configured relevance threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If the product defines no-match purely as deterministic numeric threshold on controlled vectors, Integration would be sufficient; current behavior wording is semantic/noise-based.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires real HTTP/service/runtime timing and external dependency latencies. LLM-DEP: includes real embedding and real LLM calls. MULTI-STEP: multiple agentic steps are sequenced (embedding + reranking) in one pipeline, so minimum is Workflow. PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior phrase: "end-to-end latency ... to returning re-ranked results" across pipeline including embedding and LLM rerank. Adjacent lower level miss (Agentic): a single-call agentic test misses orchestration overhead, cross-step serialization, queueing, and cumulative latency that only appears across full pipeline execution. REJECTION-GATE: none; higher level not considered beyond Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end load test against the live API path with real embedding and reranking dependencies over representative query mix; compute p50 latency and assert median < 2.0s.
